summary: Large sislib cleanup centered on naming and typing HSD_Text fields, propagating those names through game, menu, stage, lb, and un callers, and adding/improving implementations for HSD_SisLib_803A7664 and HSD_SisLib_803A75E0. The PR converted many raw offset-style HSD_Text accesses such as x0/x4/x8/x24/x30/x48/x49/x4A/x4D/x5C into semantic fields like pos_x, font_size, text_color, default_fitting, default_kerning, default_alignment, hidden, and sis_buffer. It also replaced several U8Vec4 color uses with GXColor in Pokémon Stadium text code. The bot reported one new match, HSD_SisLib_803A7664 at 100%, and an improvement for HSD_SisLib_803A75E0 to 90.91%, with a tiny percentage regression in HSD_SisLib_803A6B98.
what_changed: Renamed many HSD_Text fields in sislib.h from raw offsets to semantic names: x0/x4/x8 became pos_x/pos_y/pos_z, xC/x10 became box_size_x/box_size_y, x24 became font_size, x2C/x30 became bg_color/text_color, x48/x49/x4A became default_fitting/default_kerning/default_alignment, x4D became hidden, x4F became font_idx, x50/x54 became next/entity, x58 became render_callback, x5C became sis_buffer, x64 became alloc_data, x68 became string_buffer, x70/x74 became current_width/current_height, x8C became active_color, and x9C/x9D/x9E became fitting/kerning/alignment.
Added a temporary sisLib_803A7664_t struct in sislib.h with a todo noting it is similar to an existing allocation-data struct and that proper typing/merging remains unresolved.
Changed HSD_SisLib_803A7664 and HSD_SisLib_803A75E0 prototypes from UNK_T-based parameters to HSD_Text* parameters.
Implemented HSD_SisLib_803A7664 by operating on arg0->alloc_data, resetting data->x0 to data->x4, zeroing the first byte, and clearing data->xC.

Added an HSD_SisLib_803A75E0 implementation that checks HSD_SisLib_803A70A0, reads arg0->text_color, finds a SIS record through arg0->sis_buffer, and writes color bytes into the returned data; the diff includes a todo about not yet knowing how to model the addi 0x5 offset cleanly.

Updated sislib.c internals to use the renamed HSD_Text fields throughout rendering, string-buffer, font-index, kerning, fitting, alignment, active-color, current-width, current-height, position, and SIS-buffer logic.

Propagated HSD_Text field names through many callers in game-mode and menu code, including gm_1601, gm_1ADD, gmcamera, gmprogressive, gmresult, gmtitle, gmtou, lb_0192, mncharsel, mninfo, mninfobonus, mnmain, mnlanguage, mnsoundtest, and un_2FC91/94/95.
In grstadium.c, converted multiple text color temporaries and constants from U8Vec4 to GXColor and updated component access from x/y/z/w to r/g/b/a where appropriate.
Preserved behavior while improving readability: examples include text->font_size.x/y in place of x24.x/y, text->text_color.r/g/b/a in place of x30.x/y/z/w, and text->hidden in place of x4D.
smart_moves: The PR used broad call-site propagation after renaming HSD_Text fields, which makes the struct meanings searchable across the whole codebase instead of leaving semantic names confined to sislib.h.
Replacing U8Vec4 with GXColor in text-color contexts aligned the type with GX APIs such as GXSetTevColor and made color component names self-documenting.

Default-vs-current text state was separated by naming pairs such as default_kerning/kerning, default_fitting/fitting, and default_alignment/alignment, matching the observed pattern where default fields feed active render settings.
The HSD_SisLib_803A7664 implementation was small and strongly evidence-based: it typed the argument as HSD_Text*, then operated through alloc_data rather than preserving an opaque UNK_T interface.

The author left explicit todos where evidence was incomplete, notably around sisLib_803A7664_t being a temporary generic allocation-data shape and HSD_SisLib_803A75E0 needing a better struct model for an offset inside fn_803A6FEC results.
decomp_lessons: Once a shared struct field is understood, rename the field in the header and update all callers in the same PR so future matching work can reason from semantic access patterns rather than raw offsets.
Use API context to infer types: color data used with GX functions and RGBA byte components should be modeled as GXColor when supported by evidence.
Separate field names for initializer/default state from active/render state can clarify renderer control flow and avoid conflating values copied during text rendering.
Do not overstate uncertain struct layout: the PR kept temporary or generic names and added todos where fn_803A6FEC return layout and allocation-data equivalence were not fully proven.
Tightening prototypes from UNK_T to domain types such as HSD_Text* can unlock matching and readability when the call sites consistently pass that type.
naming_conventions: Use pos_x, pos_y, and pos_z for HSD_Text world position floats.
Use box_size_x and box_size_y for HSD_Text text-box dimensions passed to HSD_SisLib_803A5ACC.
Use font_size for the HSD_Text Vec2 that controls text scale.
Use bg_color, text_color, and active_color for GXColor-like color fields.
Use default_fitting, default_kerning, and default_alignment for default per-text render settings, and fitting, kerning, and alignment for active/current render settings.
Use hidden for the HSD_Text visibility flag instead of raw x4D.
Use sis_buffer, string_buffer, alloc_data, render_callback, next, and entity instead of raw pointer offsets when their roles are known.
Use GXColor component names r/g/b/a rather than vector component names x/y/z/w for colors.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Bot results reported HSD_SisLib_803A7664 as a new 100% match worth 32 bytes.
Bot results reported HSD_SisLib_803A75E0 improved to 90.91%, gaining 119 bytes in unmatched-function progress.

HSD_SisLib_803A75E0 still had a documented matching/layout issue around producing an addi 0x5 access to data returned by fn_803A6FEC, suggesting that a more precise record/command struct may be needed.
The PR accepted a tiny reported percentage regression in HSD_SisLib_803A6B98, from 51.50% to 51.49%, while still improving sislib overall.
Type and field-name cleanup can be matching-relevant: changing HSD_SisLib_803A7664/HSD_SisLib_803A75E0 signatures from UNK_T to HSD_Text* coincided with the reported new match and improvement.

review_feedback.feedback: GALE01 report: matched code remained 45.75% overall, with one new match HSD_SisLib_803A7664 at 100%, one improvement HSD_SisLib_803A75E0 to 90.91%, and one minor regression in HSD_SisLib_803A6B98 from 51.50% to 51.49%.
No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that refine sisLib_803A7664_t or merge it with sislib_UnkAllocData.
Search for later work on fn_803A6FEC return structs or SIS command-record layouts, especially the HSD_SisLib_803A75E0 addi 0x5 todo.
Check whether remaining HSD_Text fields x14, x34, x3C, x44, x46, x4B, x4C, x4E, x60, x6C, x6E, x78, x80, x88, x90, x92, x94, x98, and x9F were named in subsequent sislib PRs.
Look for follow-up matching improvements or regressions in HSD_SisLib_803A6B98 after the field rename sweep.
Search for other U8Vec4-to-GXColor conversions in UI/text rendering code.
